subject: [PATCH v7 02/31] mm: page_alloc: propagate PageReported flag across buddy splits

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author responded to a suggestion from Michael Tsirkin that instead of passing the was_reported flag, they should take a temporary copy of all page flags and pass them in. Author sarcastically referenced a previous instance where this approach was taken.
reviews.raw_body:
You do this exact thing again later :]

https://lore.kernel.org/linux-mm/9a22e0f9bbe1278913754db6df76e291a006181a.1778616612.git.mst@redhat.com/

~Gregory

reviews.author: Michael Tsirkin
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch is inconsistent in handling PageReported flag across buddy splits, specifically pointing out that 'Just zeroed' does not apply to this stage in the series.
reviews.raw_body:
I mean yes, reported and zeroed) Just zeroed does not exist at this
stage in the series.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author is addressing feedback about passing page flags through the buddy split process, suggesting it might be more efficient to pass them all at once and let expand() handle checking instead of individually passing each flag.
reviews.raw_body:
More of a forward looking question: if we already know we're adding
was_reported and was_zeroed, maybe we should just pass the page flags
through entirely and let expand() check them instead of passing it
through individually.

We end up doing something similar with post_alloc_hook later as well, so
I'm wondering if there's a general improvement that can be made here.

~Gregory

reviews.author: Michael Tsirkin
reviews.summary: Reviewer Michael Tsirkin asked if the patch should use a bitwise operation for type safety instead of a long, and requested clarification on the desired approach.
reviews.raw_body:
sure, will do. do you want a bitwise thing for type safety? or just a
long?

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch needs to handle PG_reported and PG_zeroed flags in the expand function, suggested a possible implementation using stable_page_flags(page) and test_bit() macros.

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch propagates the PageReported flag across buddy splits in the page allocation code, ensuring that the flag is correctly updated when pages are split or merged.
